title: Protocol for genome-wide analysis of palindrome formation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tzmxw","text":"Earlier work from our laboratory focused on identifying regions of the genome susceptible to DNA palindrome formation, a rate-limiting step in gene amplification. We described a method to obtain a genome-wide analysis of palindrome formation (GAPF) based on the efficient intrastrand base pairing in large palindromic sequences1. Palindromic sequences can rapidly anneal intramolecularly to form ‘snap-back’ DNA under conditions that do not favor intermolecular annealing. This snap-back property is used to enrich for palindromic sequences in total genomic DNA by denaturing the DNA at 100˚C, rapidly renaturing it by cooling, and then digesting the mixture with the single-strand specific nuclease S1. Snap-back DNA formed from palindromes is double-stranded and resistant to S1, whereas the remainder of genomic DNA is single-stranded and thus is sensitive to S1 digestion. Using GAPF, we have shown that de novo palindromes can form in cancers, and direct molecular analysis validated that a subset of these GAPF-positive signals represent cancer-specific palindromes located at the boundary of gene amplicons1,2. We recently discovered that the original GAPF protocol also enriches for differentially methylated DNA3. We now describe a modification of GAPF to increase the stringency of denaturation using 50% formamide to make the assay specific for DNA palindromes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":327,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":1113,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":1220,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Bioanalyzer (Agilent)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: A total of 2 micrograms of genomic DNA is used as input into the GAPF assay.
Split sample evenly into two 0.2 ml thin-walled PCR tubes.

Using manufacturer's supplied buffers, digest 1 µg of genomic DNA with KpnI (10 U) in final volume of 20 µl. In the other tube, digest 1 µg with SbfI (10 U) in a final volume of 20 µl. Incubate at 37˚C for 6 hours.
Heat inactivate restriction enzymes by incubating samples at 65˚C for 20 minutes. May freeze sample (-20˚C) at this point
From above step, combine restriction digestions into one tube (final volume = 40 µl).
Add 3 µl of 3M NaCl, 45 µl formamide, and 2 µl water to tube. May make master mix if performing multiple reactions.
Place in thermocycler preheated to 100˚C. Incubate for 7 minutes to denature DNA.
Remove tube from thermocycler and immediately place in ice-water bath. Incubate for 7 minutes.

To sample from previous step, add 8 µl 3M NaCl, 12 µl 10x S1 nuclease buffer, 8 µl water and 1 µl S1 nuclease (200 U/µl). May make master mix if performing multiple reactions. Note: Dilute stock of S1 nuclease (~1000 U/µl) in Dilution buffer to obtain 200 U/µl.
Incubate at 37˚C for 60 minutes.

For most consistent results, use ChargeSwitch PCR Clean-Up Kit (Invitrogen) to purify DNA. Alternatively, one may purify DNA using phenol:chloroform extraction followed by ethanol precipitation (use glycogen as carrier). Resuspend DNA pellet in 84 µl 1/10 TE.
Transfer S1 digested DNA to 1.7 ml microfuge tube.
Add 120 μl Purification Buffer (N5) and mix.
Add 10 μl Magnetic Beads and mix by pipetting up and down gently.
Incubate 1 minute.
Place sample on MagnaRack for 1 minute to pellet beads.
Without removing tube from MagnaRack, remove and discard supernatant.
Remove tube from MagnaRack.
Add 150 μl of Wash Buffer.
Pipet up and down gently to mix.
Place sample on MagnaRack for 1 minute to pellet beads.
Without removing tube from MagnaRack, remove and discard supernatant.
Remove tube from MagnaRack.
Add 150 μl of Wash Buffer.
Pipet up and down gently to mix.
Place sample on MagnaRack for 1 minute to pellet beads.
Without removing tube from MagnaRack, remove and discard supernatant.
Remove tube from MagnaRack.
Add 84 μl of Elution Buffer (E5).
Pipet up and down gently to mix.
Incubate 1 minute.
Place sample on MagnaRack for 1 minute to pellet beads.

Without removing tube from MagnaRack, remove and save sample. Discard beads. A total of 80 µl will be used for next step in protocol. The leftover 4 µl can be used for PCR-based enrichment assays for quality control or troubleshooting. May freeze sample (-20˚C) at this point

Restriction digestion Using manufacturer's supplied buffers, digest 40 µl of purified DNA from previous step with MseI (10 U) in final volume of 50 µl, and digest the other 40 µl of purified DNA with MspI (40 U) in final volume of 50 µl.
Incubate for 37˚C for 2 hours.
Heat inactivate MseI digest by incubating at 65˚C for 20 minutes; MspI digest requires incubation at 80˚C for 20 minutes for inactivation. May freeze sample (-20˚C) at this point

Linker ligation Add the following to the 50 µl restriction digest from previous step: 5 µl of appropriate linker (MseI or MspI linker), 7 µl 10x T4 DNA Ligase buffer, 7 µl water, and 1 µl T4 DNA ligase (400 U/µl).
Incubate at 16˚C for 3 hours.
Heat inactivate ligase by incubating at 70˚C for 20 minutes.
Remove unligated linkers To Amicon Ultracel-50 (50 kDa cutoff) Membrane 0.5 mL column, add 160 µl 1/10 TE and entire ligation mix (70 µl of either MseI or MspI ligation) from previous step.
Spin 14,000 x g for 5-10 minutes until volume concentrated to ~20 µl.
Recover DNA by placing inverted column into new tube and spinning at 1,000 x g for 2 minutes.

PCR amplification Set up the following PCR reaction: 4 µl template DNA (from above) 10 µl 10x PCR Buffer 10 µl 2 mM dNTPs 12 µl Linker specific primer (10 pmol/μl; use either JW-102gMse or JW-102gMsp) 0.8 µl FastStart Taq (Roche) 20 µl 5x GC-rich solution 43.2 µl water

Cycling conditions 96˚C 6 minutes Then 30 cycles of: 96˚C 30 seconds 55˚C 30 seconds 72˚C 30 seconds Then 72˚C 7 minutes 4˚C Hold Run 1 μl of 100 μl PCR sample on agarose gel for quality control (Average size range of Mse PCR 300-800 bp; Msp PCR 200-600 bp).
PCR clean-up To Amicon Ultracel-30 (30 kDa cutoff) Membrane 0.5 mL column, add 300 µl 1/10 TE and combined MseI and MspI PCR samples (~198 µl) from previous step.
Spin 14,000 x g for 20-30 minutes until volume concentrated to ~20 µl.
Recover DNA by placing inverted column into new tube and spinning at 1,000 x g for 2 minutes.
Quantitate DNA by Nanodrop. Should get a little greater than 7.5 µg in total. May freeze sample (-20˚C) at this point

Perform in 0.2 mL PCR tube in a thermocycler: 42.2 µl DNA (7.5 μg from above in water to 42.2 µl final volume) 4.8 µl 10x DNase I buffer 1 µl Diluted DNase I (0.0167 U; NEB) (Dilute 2 U/μl stock => 1 μl stock + 107 μl water + 12 μl 10x DNase I buffer) 37°C for 35 minutes 95°C for 15 minutes 4˚C hold 3 µl of DNA may be used on Bioanalyzer (Agilent) to measure DNA fragmentation profile for quality control purposes. Results of the Bioanalyzer analysis of fragmented DNA should show that the majority of fragmented DNA is between 25 to 200 bases, with the peak of the distribution between 25 to 100 bases.

Perform in 0.2 mL PCR tube in a thermocycler; Affy GeneChip WT Double-stranded Target Labeling Kit). 45 µl Fragmented DNA (from above) 12 µl 5x TdT buffer 2 µl TdT 1 µl DNA labeling reagent 37°C for 1 hour 70°C for 10 minutes 4˚C hold Sample now ready for hybridization to Tiling array and processing per Affymetrix protocol
2-3 days.

Genome-wide analysis of palindrome formation by Scott J Diede, Hisashi Tanaka, Donald A Bergstrom, Meng-Chao Yao & Stephen J Tapscott published in Nature Genetics on 13 Nov, 2010 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ng0410-279
creator: Protocols Importer
